compiled_state.title: Create complexity-gate.md shared prompt for evidence-backed complexity evaluation
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED (implementation half — Task 8307-3cd0 created the RED test; this task makes the file-existence and content assertions pass; consumer-reference assertions still fail until Tasks 5b, 6, 7)

## What to Build
Create `plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md`.

## Required Sections and Content

### ## Purpose
One sentence: This gate evaluates whether a proposed complexity (library dependency, new abstraction, or performance optimization) is warranted by confirmed requirements or evidence — not hypothetical future needs.

### ## Output Format
Document GATE/CHECKED/FINDING/VERDICT structured output with a concrete example:
```
GATE: Rule of Three
CHECKED: Does this abstraction have 3+ existing call sites?
FINDING: Only 1 call site found (TicketService.create). No existing reuse.
VERDICT: FAIL — abstraction not warranted. Use inline logic.
```

### ## Gate 1: YAGNI
Block complexity added in anticipation of future needs not present in the current ticket's done definitions. VERDICT: FAIL if the proposed feature/abstraction addresses a requirement absent from the current story.

### ## Gate 2: Rule of Three
No new abstraction (base class, protocol, factory, shared utility) unless the pattern appears in 3+ existing confirmed call sites in the codebase. A single planned future use does not satisfy this gate. VERDICT: FAIL if fewer than 3 existing call sites.

### ## Gate 3: Dependency Cost/Benefit
A new library dependency must satisfy: (a) functionality cannot be replicated in ≤30 lines of straightforward code, OR (b) the library provides a correctness/security guarantee not achievable with custom code. State the line count estimate and why the library wins. VERDICT: FAIL if neither condition is met.

### ## Gate 4: Scale Threshold
A performance optimization (caching, indexing, async processing, batch) must be justified by a scale estimate from scale-inference.md or a profiling result showing measured degradation. Default when scale-inference yields no estimate: "small scale" — FAIL for any performance optimization without evidence. VERDICT: FAIL if no scale evidence or profiling result is cited.

### ## Gate 5: Profiling-First
No performance optimization without a measured baseline. Cite the profiling tool, metric, and threshold that was exceeded. VERDICT: FAIL if the optimization is proposed without citing a benchmark.

### ## Gate 6: LLM Self-Audit
Structured self-check to run before proposing any complex approach:
- "Is this complexity serving a requirement in the current story's done definitions, or am I solving a hypothetical future problem?"
- "What is the simplest implementation that satisfies all done definitions and passes all tests?"
- "If I were a reviewer, what would I flag as over-engineered here?"
VERDICT: The self-audit does not produce PASS/FAIL — it produces a reflection that must be included in the proposal rationale.

### ## Justified-Complexity Path
When a gate fails but complexity IS warranted, document: (1) which gate was checked, (2) what evidence overrides the gate (scale data, security requirement, correctness guarantee, explicit user direction), (3) why the simpler alternative cannot satisfy the requirement.

### ## Sandbagging Prohibition
When a simple baseline option is required, it must represent a genuinely viable implementation path. Do not describe a technically inadequate option with scalability caveats unless those caveats are grounded in Phase 1 scale evidence. A strawman simple option exists only to make the complex option look better — this is prohibited.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md` exists and is non-empty
  Verify: `test -s plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md`
- [ ] Has GATE/CHECKED/FINDING/VERDICT format with example
  Verify: `grep -q "GATE:" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md && grep -q "VERDICT:" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md`
- [ ] Has all 6 gates (YAGNI, Rule of Three, Dependency Cost, Scale Threshold, Profiling, LLM Self-Audit)
  Verify: `grep -qi "YAGNI" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md && grep -qi "Rule of Three" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md && grep -qi "Dependency Cost\|Dependency cost" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md && grep -qi "Scale Threshold\|Scale threshold" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md && grep -qi "Profiling\|profiling" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md && grep -qi "Self-Audit\|Self-audit\|LLM self" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md`
- [ ] Has justified-complexity path section
  Verify: `grep -qi "Justified-Complexity\|justified.*complexity\|justified-complexity" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md`
- [ ] Has sandbagging prohibition
  Verify: `grep -qi "Sandbagging\|sandbagging\|strawman\|straw man" plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/complexity-gate.md`
- [ ] test-complexity-gate-shared-prompt.sh test_complexity_gate_file_exists and test_complexity_gate_required_content sections pass; consumer-reference sections still fail (expected — RED markers remain)
  Verify: `bash tests/skills/test-complexity-gate-shared-prompt.sh 2>&1 | grep -c "PASS"` — expect 8+ passes (file + content assertions)
- [ ] `plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci` passes
  Verify: `plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci`

comments.body: AC amendment (gap analysis): Add a disambiguation note in complexity-gate.md distinguishing it from complexity-evaluator.md. The note should clarify: complexity-gate.md = shared prompt for inline LLM-based complexity threshold check (used in brainstorm/implementation-plan); complexity-evaluator.md = named sub-agent definition (dispatched via dso:complexity-evaluator). Add AC: verify complexity-gate.md contains a disambiguation comment in the header.
comments.author: Test
